sub_pixel_system.notes: Fighting game on SNES; no sub-pixel movement system documented

special_mechanics.name: Chain Combos
special_mechanics.description: Chain combos could be started by using a jump punch (vertical or angled) or a vertical jump kick, creating more opportunities to use combos. Combos that knock opponents in the air no longer send one's opponent to the level above in multi-layered levels; only regular uppercuts do this.

special_mechanics.name: Stage Fatality - Scorpion's Lair
special_mechanics.description: An uppercut can send the opponent into a river of lava

special_mechanics.name: Multi-Layered Level Transitions
special_mechanics.description: Only regular uppercuts send opponents to the level above in multi-layered levels; other combo knockups do not transition levels

tier_notes: T2 empirical/qualitative data. Sources provide game design and feature documentation but contain no numerical physics parameters. Game is a fighting game ported to SNES from arcade; movement parameters typical of 16-bit fighting games but undocumented in available sources.
sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Ultimate Mortal Kombat 3 (SNES)
